behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: manifest parser/resolver -> vulnerability-check input. MOCK-MISS: a unit test of only the extractor could miss dropped transitive deps during wiring. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process fixtures and stubbed vuln client are sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior text is about "extracted...and included" correctness, not real HTTP/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner modules together on fixture manifests/lockfiles with known transitive tree; assert the outgoing vulnerability-check request set includes every expected direct/transitive dependency exactly once.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: Behavior depends on real API query semantics/contract (name+version range). MOCK-HIDE: mocked or permissive fake API can return matching fixtures even if request encodes range incorrectly, hiding real failures. REAL-INFRA: real HTTP/API behavior is part of failure boundary. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior mentions deterministic vuln DB matching, not AI model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real test/staging vulnerability API, query dependencies where same package has vulnerable and non-vulnerable versions; assert returned CVEs correspond only to manifest-specified version ranges.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: Single local ordering rule/algorithm; no module boundary required for the described failure. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none.
behaviors.test_description: Call sorter with unsorted CVE fixtures spanning severities and equal-severity CVSS ties; assert exact output order is severity rank first, then descending CVSS within each severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears across parse->mutate->serialize modules; unit-testing only mutator can miss unintended changes introduced by serializer/writer integration. REAL-INFRA: local in-process fixtures are enough (no real network/subprocess). LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior is about manifest content integrity, not OS-specific file event/process behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end manifest update pipeline on realistic manifest fixtures; diff before/after parsed dependency maps and assert only target dependency version changed.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository/PR creation; failure boundary includes git + external VCS API/network. MOCK-HIDE: mocked PR client can pass while real auth, branch, diff, or API constraints fail. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI model judgment is involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real test repository, execute scanner update flow and verify an actual PR is opened; assert PR diff contains exactly one dependency version change and no unrelated file edits.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Pure semver comparison logic in a local function/object boundary; no I/O or cross-module dependency required for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Feed version pairs covering major/minor/patch/prerelease edge cases into breaking-change classifier; assert only major bumps are flagged breaking per semver rules.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/state handoff: semver decision -> PR subsystem suppression -> output/report flag. MOCK-MISS: unit tests of each piece can miss wiring errors where PR still triggers or flag is absent. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no real OS/network needed because behavior says PR is "skipped"; (b) no AI/ML output dependency; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) one deterministic decision gate, not LLM stage orchestration. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected by concrete text "PR creation is skipped," so validating non-invocation + surfaced flag does not require real repo calls.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner flow with fixtures that force major bump; use a spy/fake PR client and real reporting module wiring; assert PR client is not invoked and review-required flag appears in emitted output.
